verdict_summary: All 6 attack surfaces were stress tested. The implemention is functionally sound: the delegate → agent type collapse at the ContentPart boundary is intentional by design, the full round-trip works correctly, and edge cases (empty state, performance) are handled. Two moderate findings and one major finding require attention before release.

attacks_attempted.description: Delegates are serialized as AgentPart with type 'agent' downstream. Test whether any consumer needs to distinguish delegates from agents.
attacks_attempted.detail: The 'delegate' type is purely a UI-level discriminator in AtOption. At addPart(), delegate selections produce {type:'agent', name} ContentParts — same as agents. The DOM pill is data-type='agent'. The API has no concept of delegate vs agent. This is by design (plan intent: delegates ARE agents for API purposes). However, if future code needs to answer 'was this mention created by selecting a subagent?', that information is permanently lost. The AgentPart type has no description field and no origin flag.

attacks_attempted.description: The @mention filter excludes mode==='primary' and hidden agents. The composer dropdown uses a different agent source. Can the same agent appear in both views with inconsistent types?
attacks_attempted.detail: agentList() filters sync.data.agent with !hidden && mode!=='primary'. Subagent-mode agents become delegates; others stay agents. The sets are disjoint per agent (single mode). BUT: the composer dropdown uses local.agent.list() which can include different agents. If a subagent has both a sync.data.agent entry AND a local.agent.list() entry, it appears in the @popover as a delegate AND in the composer dropdown as a regular agent picker option — potentially confusing users who see the same agent in two places with different labels.

attacks_attempted.surface: Empty state (@ with no agents or files)
attacks_attempted.description: What renders when user types @ and there are zero results?
attacks_attempted.detail:
Handled: 0} fallback={
{t('prompt.popover.emptyResults')}
}>. Popover has min-h-10 minimum height. If both agentList() is empty AND files.searchFilesAndDirectories returns nothing, the fallback renders 'emptyResults' text. No crash, no blank area.

attacks_attempted.surface: Keyboard navigation with 4 groups at 3 ranks
attacks_attempted.description: ArrowUp/Down navigation across delegate (rank 0), agent (rank 0), recent (rank 1), file (rank 2) groups. What happens at group boundaries?
attacks_attempted.detail: Two issues: (1) Both delegate and agent rank 0 → useFilteredList group ordering at same rank is insertion-order-dependent and potentially non-deterministic. If the groups interleave, a user arrowing down might see an agent, then a delegate, then back to an agent alphabetically with no visual group separator. (2) The popover renders a flat list with NO group headers — no 'Agents', 'Subagents', 'Files' labels separate items. Users navigating by keyboard have no contextual cue which group they're in. The only visual differentiator is the icon (brain vs share) but this requires user familiarity. Arrow-jumping from the last delegate to the first recent file (different rank) happens without warning.

attacks_attempted.description: agentList() produces 50+ AtOptions. The items() function in useFilteredList computes every keystroke. Render slices to 10.
attacks_attempted.detail: agentList() is a createMemo — re-computed only when sync.data.agent changes. The items() async callback runs on every keystroke but only computes agentList() reference (memo hit), recent() (memo hit), and optionally files.searchFilesAndDirectories(query) (async). The in-memory filter on 50 items is negligible. Render is capped at .slice(0,10). No performance concern. Tested in code review — no benchmark run.

attacks_attempted.surface: Full round-trip: @popover → pill → parseFromDOM → buildRequestParts → API → extractPromptFromParts
attacks_attempted.description: Verify delegate selections survive the complete cycle without data loss or type errors.
attacks_attempted.detail: Functional round-trip ✅: delegate → handleAtSelect → addPart({type:'agent', name:'stress'}) → createPill(data-type='agent', data-name='stress') → parseFromDOM pushes agent → buildRequestParts creates AgentPartInput(type:'agent', name:'stress') → API → extractPromptFromParts includes agent part → restore into PromptInput. The delegate name is preserved throughout. BUT: (a) the delegate description is lost immediately — AgentPart type has no description field, so the tooltip/description shown in the popover disappears. (b) createPill always sets data-type='agent' regardless of delegate origin — no data-subagent attribute exists, so after round-trip the pill renders identically to a regular agent pill. (c) pre-existing: pushAgent uses agent.dataset.name! non-null assertion — if the DOM node was created externally (paste, script), name becomes undefined silently.

findings.description: No visual group headers in @popover for delegate/agent/recent/file groups. Users navigating by keyboard have no context about which group they are in.
findings.reproduction:
1. Configure at least one subagent and one regular agent
2. Type @ in prompt input
3. ArrowDown through results
4. Observe: no 'Agents', 'Subagents', 'Recent Files', 'Files' section headers
findings.fix: Add group headers to the popover when multiple groups are present. Use the groupBy category to render section labels above the first item of each group.

findings.description: Delegate and agent groups share rank 0, making their interleaving order non-deterministic. Same-rank group ordering in useFilteredList depends on insertion order (which is agentList construction order: delegates first, then agents).
findings.reproduction:
1. Configure one subagent named 'alpha' (delegate) and one agent named 'beta'
2. Type @ in prompt input
3. Observe: items appear in insertion order (alpha then beta) NOT alphabetically across groups
4. Change agent names so an agent sorts before a delegate alphabetically
5. Observe: groups may interleave instead of staying contiguous
findings.fix: Either merge delegate and agent into a single 'people' group (same rank, same group key), or ensure delegates sort after agents using sortGroupsBy with distinct ranks (0 for agents, 0.5 for delegates).

findings.description: Delegate description is lost when the mention is committed as a ContentPart. The AgentPart type has no description field, so the tooltip shown in the popover disappears permanently.
findings.reproduction:
1. Select a subagent with a description from the @popover
2. The description shows in the popover row
3. Submit the prompt
4. When restored (undo/edit), the mention shows @name with no way to recover the description
findings.fix: If descriptions should persist, add optional description to AgentPart type and data-description attribute to the DOM pill. Otherwise, document this as intentional.

findings.description: No visual distinction between agent and delegate pills in the editor. Both render with createPill which sets data-type='agent' regardless of origin. The share icon from the popover is not preserved in the editor DOM.
findings.reproduction:
1. Select a delegate (subagent) from the @popover — shows share icon
2. After select, the pill in the editor shows '@name' as plain text
3. No visual cue that this is a delegated subagent vs a regular agent mention
findings.fix: Add a data-subagent attribute to the pill when the mention originated from a delegate, or render a different visual in the editor for delegate mentions.

findings.description: Subagents appear in both the @popover (as delegates with share icon) and potentially the composer agent dropdown (as regular agents). Users may see the same agent twice with different representations.
findings.reproduction:
1. Configure a subagent that also appears in local.agent.list()
2. Compare the @popover (shows delegate with share icon) vs the composer picker (shows as regular agent)
3. Same agent, different UI treatment and context
findings.fix: Either exclude subagent-mode agents from the composer dropdown, or use consistent representation across both surfaces.

findings.description: Delegate vs agent distinction is lost at the ContentPart level. Any future code that needs to query 'was this mention a delegate?' has no mechanism to do so.
findings.reproduction: Not reproducible currently — no consumer requires this distinction.
findings.fix: No immediate action needed. If the need arises, add a 'kind' or 'origin' field to AgentPart: { type:'agent', name, kind:'subagent'|'agent' }.
